steps.title: Find the slope from two table points
steps.content:
Using the points $(1, 5)$ and $(3, 13)$ from the table:
$$m = \frac{13 - 5}{3 - 1} = \frac{8}{2} = 4$$

steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content:
With $f(x) = 4x + b$, plug in $(1, 5)$:
$5 = 4(1) + b \Rightarrow b = 1$.
So $f(x) = 4x + 1$.

steps.title: Verify and eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
Check $(5, 21)$: $4(5) + 1 = 21$. ✓
  • A) $f(x)=2x+3$: $f(1)=5$ ✓ but $f(3)=9 eq 13$. Eliminated.
  • B) $f(x)=3x+2$: $f(1)=5$ ✓ but $f(3)=11 eq 13$. Eliminated.
  • C) $f(x)=4x+1$: $f(1)=5$, $f(3)=13$, $f(5)=21$. All match.
  • D) $f(x)=5x$: $f(1)=5$ ✓ but $f(3)=15 eq 13$. Eliminated.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
